source_pr.title: ddl: make test TestRenameTableAutoIDs more stable
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #53765

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

The root cause is the same with this one https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/54447
When we do ddl, wait ddl status change to finish is not enough.
We need to wait the infoschema reload finish, otherwise we may see the stale infoschema.

Here the test code execute `renamte table  renametableautoids.t   to  renametableautoids2.t2`
Using the stale infoschema would get error :

```
        	Error:      	Received unexpected error:
        	            	[schema:1146]Table 'renametableautoids2.t2' doesn't exist
        	            	github.com/pingcap/errors.AddStack
        	            		external/com_github_pingcap_errors/errors.go:178
```

NOTE, rename table cross different DBs have known capatibility issue with  autoid.
And even after this fix, the test may still be unstable by that capability issue.

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note

```

symptoms: And even after this fix, the test may still be unstable by that capability issue.
failure_signature: And even after this fix, the test may still be unstable by that capability issue.
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky around DDL/schema changes where schema propagation is asynchronous. This PR stabilizes it by: ddl: make test TestRenameTableAutoIDs more stable.
fix_pattern: ddl: make test TestRenameTableAutoIDs more stable

evidence: evidence: And even after this fix, the test may still be unstable by that capability issue.
